sub_modules.title: Arabian Region - Winter
sub_modules.content: Influenced by Siberian High, westerlies, and occasional thunderstorms.

sub_modules.title: Arabian Region - Summer
sub_modules.content: Baluchistan Low, intense heat, dust storms, SW monsoon influence in east.

sub_modules.title: Calcutta to Singapore - Winter
sub_modules.content: Dominated by NE monsoon with generally clear weather, increasing convection towards Malaysia.

sub_modules.title: Calcutta to Singapore - Summer
sub_modules.content: SW monsoon brings thunderstorms, heavy rain, and poor flying conditions; Sumatras occur.

sub_modules.title: Singapore to Tokyo - Winter
sub_modules.content: NE monsoon dominates; convection in south, snow in Japan, and fog (Crachin) near Hong Kong.

sub_modules.title: Singapore to Tokyo - Summer
sub_modules.content: SW monsoon, ITCZ movement, typhoons, and heavy rainfall along route.

sub_modules.title: Singapore to Auckland - Winter
sub_modules.content: SE trade winds, dry Australian interior, and polar front activity near New Zealand.

sub_modules.title: Singapore to Auckland - Summer
sub_modules.content: ITCZ, tropical cyclones, thunderstorms, and strong convection dominate.

sub_modules.title: Cairo to Johannesburg - January
sub_modules.content: Dry Sahara conditions in north; ITCZ brings thunderstorms and wet season in south.

sub_modules.title: Cairo to Johannesburg - July
sub_modules.content: ITCZ shifts north; Haboobs in Sudan; dry winter conditions in Johannesburg.

sub_modules.title: Nairobi Region
sub_modules.content: Two ITCZ passages cause Long Rains (spring) and Short Rains (autumn), with orographic cloud and thunderstorms.

sub_modules.title: Introduction to Satellite Meteorology
sub_modules.content: Satellites provide real-time weather data and images, improving forecasting and global observation.

sub_modules.title: Polar Orbiting Satellites
sub_modules.content: Low altitude satellites providing high-resolution images but not continuous coverage.

sub_modules.title: Geostationary Satellites
sub_modules.content: High altitude satellites providing continuous monitoring over a fixed region.

sub_modules.title: Visual Satellite Images
sub_modules.content: Daytime images showing clouds clearly but unavailable at night.

sub_modules.title: Infrared (IR) Images
sub_modules.content: 24-hour imaging based on temperature differences; useful for detecting cloud height.

sub_modules.title: False Colour Images
sub_modules.content: Enhanced images using colour to differentiate cloud types and temperatures.

sub_modules.title: Image Location and Grids
sub_modules.content: Use of grids and coastlines to identify positions on satellite images.

sub_modules.title: Interpretation of Satellite Images
sub_modules.content: Satellite images are best interpreted alongside synoptic charts and time-lapse sequences.

sub_modules.title: Introduction to METAR
sub_modules.content: METAR is a coded report of actual weather conditions at an aerodrome at a specific time.

sub_modules.title: Report Structure Overview
sub_modules.content: METAR consists of groups including report type, aerodrome, time, wind, visibility, weather, cloud, temperature, and pressure.

sub_modules.title: Wind Information
sub_modules.content: Includes direction, speed, gusts, and variability of wind.

sub_modules.title: Visibility and RVR
sub_modules.content: Visibility is reported in metres; RVR is included when visibility is poor.

sub_modules.title: Weather Group
sub_modules.content: Describes present weather using standardized codes for precipitation, obscuration, and phenomena.

sub_modules.title: Cloud Information
sub_modules.content: Cloud amount, height, and significant cloud types (CB, TCU) are reported.

sub_modules.title: CAVOK and Obscuration
sub_modules.content: CAVOK indicates good conditions; VV is used when sky is obscured.

sub_modules.title: Temperature and Dew Point
sub_modules.content: Reported in Celsius with dew point indicating moisture content.

sub_modules.title: QNH (Pressure)
sub_modules.content: Surface pressure reduced to sea level, reported in hPa or inches Hg.

sub_modules.title: Recent Weather (RE)
sub_modules.content: Indicates significant weather in the past hour.

sub_modules.title: Windshear (WS)
sub_modules.content: Reports presence of windshear affecting runways.

sub_modules.title: Trend Forecast
sub_modules.content: Short-term forecast using BECMG, TEMPO, or NOSIG.

sub_modules.title: Runway State Group
sub_modules.content: Provides information about runway contamination and braking conditions.

sub_modules.title: Special Reports (SPECI)
sub_modules.content: Issued when significant weather changes occur between METARs.

sub_modules.title: AUTO METAR
sub_modules.content: Automatically generated METARs with missing data indicated by slashes.

sub_modules.title: End of Message
sub_modules.content: Equals sign (=) indicates end of METAR.

sub_modules.title: Introduction to TAF
sub_modules.content: TAF is a forecast of weather conditions at an aerodrome for a specified time period.

sub_modules.title: TAF Structure Overview
sub_modules.content: TAF format includes report type, aerodrome, issue time, validity period, wind, visibility, weather, and cloud.

sub_modules.title: Date-Time Groups
sub_modules.content: Includes issue time and validity period of the forecast.

sub_modules.title: Wind, Visibility, and Weather
sub_modules.content: Same coding as METAR for wind, visibility, and weather conditions.

sub_modules.title: Cloud Information
sub_modules.content: Cloud amount and height are reported; NSC used when no significant cloud.

sub_modules.title: Forecast Change Indicators
sub_modules.content: Includes FM, BECMG, TEMPO, and PROB to indicate changes in forecast conditions.

sub_modules.title: FM (From)
sub_modules.content: Indicates rapid change with a completely new set of conditions.

sub_modules.title: BECMG (Becoming)
sub_modules.content: Indicates gradual and permanent change over a specified time period.

sub_modules.title: TEMPO (Temporary)
sub_modules.content: Indicates temporary fluctuations lasting less than one hour at a time.

sub_modules.title: PROB (Probability)
sub_modules.content: Indicates likelihood of certain weather events, usually 30% or 40%.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Forecast
sub_modules.content: Some TAFs include maximum and minimum temperature forecasts.

sub_modules.title: Amendments (AMD)
sub_modules.content: Indicates that the TAF has been updated.

sub_modules.title: End of Message
sub_modules.content: Equals sign (=) indicates the end of the TAF.

chapter_name: Chapter 27 - Significant Weather and Wind Charts

sub_modules.title: Introduction to WAFS
sub_modules.content: Global system providing aviation weather charts via WAFC London and Washington.

sub_modules.title: SIGWX Charts
sub_modules.content: Show significant weather phenomena such as turbulence, icing, jet streams, and CB clouds.

sub_modules.title: Jet Streams
sub_modules.content: Depicted with arrows and feathers showing wind speed and direction at high altitude.

sub_modules.title: Turbulence and Icing
sub_modules.content: Areas of CAT, turbulence, and icing are indicated with altitude ranges.

sub_modules.title: Cloud and Thunderstorm Areas
sub_modules.content: CB clouds and storm areas are shown with descriptors like ISOL, OCNL, FRQ, and SQL.

sub_modules.title: Tropopause and Freezing Level
sub_modules.content: Tropopause height and freezing levels are indicated on charts.

sub_modules.title: Wind and Temperature Charts
sub_modules.content: Provide wind direction, speed, and temperature at various flight levels.

sub_modules.title: Interpolation of Winds
sub_modules.content: Wind and temperature values between grid points are estimated through interpolation.

sub_modules.title: Low-Level Forecast Charts (Forms 215/415)
sub_modules.content: Provide regional forecasts including visibility, cloud, weather, and freezing levels.

sub_modules.title: Weather Coverage Terms
sub_modules.content: Definitions of ISOL, OCNL, FRQ, and WDSPR used to describe weather distribution.

sub_modules.title: Spot Wind Charts (Forms 214/414)
sub_modules.content: Provide wind and temperature data at specific locations and altitudes.

sub_modules.title: Introduction to Warning Messages
sub_modules.content: Different meteorological warnings alert pilots to hazardous conditions during flight and at aerodromes.

sub_modules.title: Aerodrome Warnings
sub_modules.content: Warnings for hazards such as strong winds, thunderstorms, snow, fog, frost, and freezing precipitation.

sub_modules.title: Windshear Warnings
sub_modules.content: Alerts for windshear conditions affecting approach and departure paths.

sub_modules.title: SIGMET
sub_modules.content: Warnings for significant en-route weather hazards such as turbulence, icing, thunderstorms, and volcanic ash.

sub_modules.title: Volcanic Ash Advisory
sub_modules.content: Messages from VAACs providing information about volcanic ash clouds and their movement.

sub_modules.title: Aviation Colour Codes
sub_modules.content: Colour-coded system indicating volcanic activity levels (Green, Yellow, Orange, Red).

sub_modules.title: Tropical Cyclone Advisory
sub_modules.content: Warnings issued by specialized centres tracking tropical cyclones and their forecast paths.

chapter_name: Chapter 29 - Meteorological Information for Aircraft in Flight

sub_modules.title: Introduction to In-Flight Weather Information
sub_modules.content: Pilots can receive real-time weather updates during flight via radio broadcasts.

sub_modules.title: VOLMET
sub_modules.content: Continuous broadcast of METARs, TAFs, and SIGMETs over VHF and HF frequencies.

sub_modules.title: VOLMET Operation
sub_modules.content: Regional stations provide sequential weather updates for multiple aerodromes.

sub_modules.title: HF VOLMET
sub_modules.content: Global VOLMET broadcasts used especially for oceanic routes like the North Atlantic.

sub_modules.title: ATIS
sub_modules.content: Continuous broadcast of current aerodrome weather and operational information.

sub_modules.title: ATIS Operation
sub_modules.content: Updated with changes and identified by alphabetical codes (Alpha, Bravo, etc.).

sub_modules.title: Use of ATIS
sub_modules.content: Pilots must listen to ATIS before contacting ATC and report the information code.
